executive_summary: The candidate demonstrates strong technical command and clear pronunciation, making their core message inherently understandable. However, delivery is hampered by excessive filler usage and a monotone vocal style, which limits the overall impact of their communication. Addressing grammatical accuracy and increasing speech speed will transform their technical proficiency into a more commanding professional presence. With targeted coaching on pacing and vocal projection, the candidate is well-positioned for roles requiring clear communication.

features.raw_text: Hi afternoon. My name is Roshan Jadhav and I am .NET full stack developer with 3.2 years of professional experience in designing and developing scalable web applications. I specialize in ASP.NET Core, .NET Core and C sharp and Angular. I have strong experience in building secure rest full Web API. Implementing micro services, architecture and working extensively with SQL Server, for database, query optimization and performance tuning. In my previous projects I worked on developing enterprise level application where I handled backend API development. Authentication using JWT and role based access control and integrated fronted using Angular. So I enjoy solving complex problems, optimizing performance and building scalable secure applications. That's it. Okay, so JWT token Access control in my project in my project I implement RBAC using JWT authentication plus role based authorization policies. After successful login, validate user credentials, fetch roles from database and add roles as claims in JWT and in my project I ensure roles stored in DB not hardcoded. So claims based approach, token expires approach, I implement role based access using JWT claims and policy based authorization, ensuring centralized control, clean architecture and secure access management across API's. That's it. Okay, in my project JWT and role based authentication approach in one my projects high traffic API system and we started facing API latency spikes and database load issues. So I applied several advanced .NET features and architectural patterns to improve performance and scalability and reduced DB calls, lower response time and reduced CPU load on SQL Server. Then I used entity frame work core optimization, used not as no tracking for read only queries and fixed the N+1 query issues using .include and add proper indexing used the projection instead of fetching full entity then dependent injection used a proper lifetime management and scoped for DB context and Singleton for caching services. For heavy operations we used emails and reports. Move to background jobs instead of blocking request thread. Improved user response time significantly and we use middleware for centralized exception handling, reduce duplicate try catch blocks and improvement maintainability. I focus on async programming, caching, EF core optimization and clean architecture patterns to both performance and scalability in production applications. That's it. Okay, entity framework code tuning and background jobs like whenever I implement performance optimization, I make sure they do not compromise clean architecture or testability. So I follow clean architecture layered architecture. Controller only handles HTTP and services handle business logic and repository is handled data access. So performance changes like caching or entity framework optimization are applied inside the service or repository layer, not directly in controllers. and used interface everywhere. Even if I introduce caching or optimization the controller doesn't change. Easy mocking, unit testing and swapping implementations without breaking and even after optimization, unit testing business logic, integration test API endpoints validate behavior remains same. So I ensure performance optimizations are implemented inside proper layer using interfaces and dependency injection so that the system remains modular, testable and easy to maintain. That's it. Okay, object oriented programming principles in my projects. First I follow the single responsibility principles where each class has only one responsibility. For example controls, controllers handle http requests. Service content business logic and repository manages database operations. This improves maintainability and testability. Second I use open close principles, meaning classes are open for extension but closed for modification. I achieve this by using interfaces and abstraction. For example, if I need to change payment logic, I create a new implementation without modifying extension code. So third I follow list substitution principle by ensuring derive classes can replace base classes without breaking functionality. Fourth I follow interface principle where I create small specific interfaces instead of large general ones. This makes the system loosely coupled. Fifth I use dependency injection principle by depending on abstractions instead of concrete classes. In .NET I use built in dependency injection to inject services into controllers. This improves flexibility and unit testings. That's it. Okay. Okay feature based first feature based model structure, I organize the application into feature model like user model, product model, dashboard model. Each model had smart components reusable. So I used smart and dumb components approach to smart components handle API calls and business logic and dumb components were reusable UI components that receives data using @ input and @ output also. I implemented reactive forms for complex forms with proper validations. So I used angular services to call backend API's implemented routing with lazy loading for performance optimization and used interceptors to attach JWT tokens to every HTTP request. And for integration with the .NET backend services we consumed rest API's built using ASP.NET Core and used HTTP client for API communication. So handle authentication using JWT token stored securely and implement proper error handling using global error inspector. That's it. Okay. In my project interceptors and forms manage state based on the application complexity. I follow clean and scalable approach service based state, medium application. This allows centralized state and reactive updates and easy communication between components. So I use this and smart and dumb components pattern smart container components are handled API calls and manage states and manage state. So and state management with NG RX. So and dump presentational components I use receive data via @ input and email events via @ output. This keeps UI clean and maintainable and NG RX means for large applications in larger applications with complex states. So I prepare NG RX and store actions reducers effects. So and also in my project I used shared service with behavioral subject for maintaining login state and user data across modules. For complex forms I use reactive forms with strong validation. So I ensured separation of concerns using smart and dump components which made the application scalable and easy to maintain. That's it. Okay. Between Azure App Service and Azure functions like Azure App Service Service is a fully managed platform to host a web applications rest API and back end services and it is best for ASP.NET Core MVC application web API and full stack applications. runs Azure App Service runs continuously and manual or auto scale rules and supports deployment slots and support custom domains and SSL. Good for stateful or complex application and Azure functions are is an event driving serverless compute service where you run small piece of code in response to event and it's best for background jobs and event driving processing shadow tasks, QA processing and lightweight API's. So fully serverless and no server management automatically scales based on demand and pay only for education time. So trigger based like HTTP timer QA blob event tab. If I need to send email after user registration or process a file upload so I would use Azure function triggered by QA or HTTP request. That's it. Okay. Azure Active Directory known as Microsoft Inter ID is a cloud base identity and access management service. So it helps manage authentication and authorization for users and application in Azure. Its main role is to provide secure identity Management for cloud applications. First it handles authentication when a user tries to access an application hosted on Azure App Service or any cloud resource, Azure AD verifies the user identity using credential like username and password. Multi factor authentication after successful login it issues a secure token such as the JWT token. Second it manages authorization using role based access control Azure AD ensures that users can only access resources they are permitted to roles like owner, contributor or reader can be assigned it. Resource group or resource level. It supports single sign on like SSO users can login once and access multiple applications without logging. It enhances security with features like multi factor authentication conditional access policies, identity protection and managed identities for secure communication between Azure services. That's it. Okay. Azure SQL Database supports before explaining Azure SQL in High Availability ensures that database remains available even if hardware of node failure occurs and Azure SQL database automatically maintains multiple replaces of the database within the same region. In the general purpose and business critical tires it uses replica based architecture wherein general purpose compute and storage are separated and storage has been in redundancy. In business critical multiple replaces are maintained using always on availability technology. Disaster recovery means Azure SQL database supports and active your reflection, it allows you to create readable secondary databases in different Azure regions. So in case of regional failure, you can manually fail over to another region. Second add to failover groups, this provides automatic geo failure between primary and secondary regions with a single endpoint for application. So it reduces down time significantly. And automated backups, Azure automatically takes full and differential and transaction log backups. So you can restore the database to any point in time with the reduction period and fourth one is a long term back in retention. So it is used for complex and archival purpose. So that's it.
